meta.home: Irtaqi — Rise through your daily worship

hero.title: Rise through your daily worship
hero.verse: “So race toward all that is good.”
hero.subtitle: Build the habits of a lifetime — 63 acts of worship, 12 spiritual levels, and a companion that grows with you.

homePillars.title: Built around three pillars
homePillars.subtitle: Every feature in Irtaqi serves one of three goals — nearness to Allah, consistency, and community.

taqwa.title: Taqwa
taqwa.body: God-consciousness through the five daily prayers, morning and evening adhkar, and the sunnah rawatib.

ibadah.title: Ibadah
ibadah.body: A structured ward for the Qur'an, fasting, charity, and the weekly, monthly, and yearly acts of devotion.

ihsan.title: Ihsan
ihsan.body: Self-accountability, character, and the inner stations of the heart — from repentance to certainty.

homePreview.title: A real companion, not a mockup.
homePreview.body: Native right-to-left Arabic typography. Your station, your streak, and today's points — all in one place, with the Friday ×1.5 multiplier visible at the top. This is your home screen, from a working app.
homePreview.imageAlt: Irtaqi home screen in Arabic, showing the Tawbah station and the Friday multiplier

homeTour.title: A short tour of the app
homeTour.subtitle: Every screen here is from a working app — no mockups.
homeTour.dailyAlt: Irtaqi daily worship list — fard prayers and sunan
homeTour.dailyCaption: Daily worship — the five prayers and their sunan and adhkar, tagged Fard or Sunnah Muakkadah.
homeTour.weeklyAlt: Irtaqi weekly worship view — Jumu'ah, Ghusl, Surat al-Kahf
homeTour.weeklyCaption: Weekly worship — Jumu'ah prayer, ghusl, Surat al-Kahf, and the encouraged sending of salutations on the Prophet ﷺ.
homeTour.monthlyAlt: Irtaqi monthly worship view — Ayyam Beed fasting, monthly sadaqah, khatm of the Qur'an
homeTour.monthlyCaption: Monthly worship — completing a khatm of the Qur'an, monthly sadaqah, fasting the white days, visiting kin.
homeTour.groupsAlt: Irtaqi groups screen with two groups and a leaderboard
homeTour.groupsCaption: Small groups — private leaderboard, group challenges, and an invite code to bring others in.

homeProblem.title: Why is consistency so hard?
homeProblem.body: Without structure and reminders, even sincere intentions drift. Most of us want to hold onto our daily worship, but life moves fast and the habit slips before we notice.

homeSolution.title: Irtaqi — tracking meets faith-based motivation
homeSolution.body: Track your daily worship. See your streak. Rise through the twelve classical stations. Earn recognition anchored in verses and hadith — not novelty, but nearness.

homeLevels.title: 12 stations — from Repentance to Certainty
homeLevels.body: Inspired by the classical maqamat literature. Each station is anchored in a Qur'anic verse. Progress is earned through daily consistency.

homeBadges.title: 18 badges — across three pillars
homeBadges.body: Recognition for milestones on the path of Taqwa, Ibadah, and Ihsan — from forty consecutive Fajrs to completing a full khatm of the Qur'an.

homeFree.title: Free forever. No ads. Volunteer project.
homeFree.body: Irtaqi is built by Muslims, for Muslims, funded only by optional donations. No ads. No subscriptions. No selling your worship data. Every feature is free for everyone, for as long as the project exists.
homeFree.verseEn: “Spend from that which He has made you successors.”

homeCta.title: Your journey to Ihsan starts with one habit.
homeCta.body: Pick the acts of worship you want to commit to. Track them daily. Rise, level by level.

featuresGallery.title: Here's what it looks like inside
featuresGallery.subtitle: Snapshots from a build in active testing — details shift, the message doesn't.

aboutPreview.title: One face, one purpose
aboutPreview.body: Your home in Irtaqi gathers everything in one place: the station you've reached, today's points, and today's worship. Arabic-first throughout, and every seasonal feature — like the Friday multiplier shown here — is anchored in a textual source.
aboutPreview.imageAlt: Irtaqi home screen showing the user's name, the Tawbah station, and the Friday multiplier

downloadGallery.title: Take a look before you download

features.title: Everything you need to grow
features.subtitle: Irtaqi is designed by Muslims, for Muslims — rooted in the Qur'an and the Sunnah.

habits.title: 63 Islamic habits
habits.body: A curated library spanning prayers, adhkar, Qur'an, ethics, and the weekly, monthly, and yearly acts — each tagged by type (fard, sunnah, mustahab) and weighted by points.

levels.title: 12 spiritual stations
levels.body: Progress through twelve maqamat — from Tawbah (Repentance) to Yaqin (Certainty) — each anchored in a verse from the Qur'an.

badges.title: 18 achievement badges
badges.body: Earn recognition across three pillars — Taqwa, Ibadah, and Ihsan — for milestones like 40 consecutive Fajrs or completing a khatm of the Qur'an.

streaks.title: Streaks and stats
streaks.body: Daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly views. Track your current streak, your longest streak, and your total points over time.

groups.title: Groups and challenges
groups.body: Build a small circle of family or friends. Compete on a private leaderboard — and let the group admin launch a shared challenge: pick the worship goals, set a deadline, and watch a live per-member progress board. “Race toward all that is good.”

madhab.title: Madhab-aware
madhab.body: Choose your madhab — Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i, or Hanbali — and Irtaqi tailors prayer times, sunnah counts, and juristic nuances accordingly.

bilingual.title: Arabic and English
bilingual.body: Fully bilingual with native RTL support. Arabic scripture is rendered in Amiri; the UI uses Cairo — chosen for clarity at every size.

offline.title: Private and encrypted
offline.body: Your data lives in your account and syncs securely over HTTPS. A local cache keeps the app fast and usable offline. No ads, no tracking, no selling your worship to anyone.

fiqhDetails.title: Fiqh detail on every habit
fiqhDetails.body: Tap ⓘ on any habit to see the rakat count, what to recite, its virtue from Qur'an and hadith, and madhhab variants — all in one place, sourced, not invented.

challengeFeature.title: Group challenges
challengeFeature.body: The group admin picks worship goals and sets a deadline. Members compete to complete them — with a live per-member progress leaderboard. “And in that let those who compete, compete.” (83:26)

quranReader.title: Built-in Qur'an reader
quranReader.body: Browse the full Mushaf inside the app in Amiri script. Jump directly to any surah, search by name, and navigate pages by swipe or button.

qibla.title: Qibla compass
qibla.body: Find the direction of Makkah with a live compass in Settings. Uses the device sensor in real time — shows the bearing in degrees and the great-circle distance to the Masjid al-Haram.

dhikrCounter.title: Dhikr counter
dhikrCounter.body: A built-in tap counter for repetitive worship — Istighfar 100×, Salawat, Tasbeeh. Advances automatically through the Tasbeeh cycle (SubhanAllah 33, Alhamdulillah 33, Allahu Akbar 34) and logs the habit on completion.

dailyReminder.title: Daily Qur'anic reminder
dailyReminder.body: A rotating ayah or hadith at the top of the home screen every day — 32 reminders drawn from the Qur'an and authentic Sunnah, changing daily and consistent throughout the day.

adhanSound.title: Adhan notification sound
adhanSound.body: Choose your prayer reminder sound: a short adhan (Hayya 'ala as-Salah, Hayya 'ala al-Falah), a simple chime, or the device default — switchable from Settings at any time.

smartHome.title: Smart home screen
smartHome.body: Weekly, monthly, and yearly worship appears automatically on the right day: Jumu'ah and Surat al-Kahf on Friday, White Days fasting on Hijri 13-14-15, and Ramadan and Dhul-Hijja acts in their seasons.

about.title: About Irtaqi
about.lede: Irtaqi (Arabic: ارتقِ — “rise”) is an Islamic habit tracker built around a simple idea: consistency is the shortest path to nearness.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.body: To help Muslims build — and keep — the daily habits that shape a life of worship. A serious companion rooted in the Qur'an, the Sunnah, and the classical tradition of self-accountability (muhasaba).

one.title: Rooted in tradition
one.body: Every habit, every badge, every level is drawn from Qur'anic verses and authentic hadith — not invented for engagement.

two.title: Small, daily, consistent
two.body: “The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small.” — our product philosophy, literally.

three.title: Privacy by default
three.body: Your ibadah is between you and your Lord. We don't need to see it, sell it, or mine it.

verse.en: “And give glad tidings to the patient.”

download.title: Download Irtaqi
download.subtitle: Available on iOS and Android. Arabic and English.

launch.title: Not out yet
launch.body: Irtaqi is in development. Leave your email and we'll let you know the moment it's live — nothing else.

contact.title: Get in touch
contact.subtitle: Questions, feedback, a hadith we missed — send it our way.

form.name: Your name
form.subject: Subject
form.success: Thanks — your message has been received.
form.error: Please fill in all fields with a valid email.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Irtaqi is designed to respect your privacy. This policy explains what we collect, what we don't, and what we'll never do.

collect.title: What we collect
collect.body: Only what's needed to make the app work: your email and password (hashed with bcrypt), the habits you choose to track and your completion logs, groups you create or join, and optional profile fields (name, avatar, madhhab, location for prayer times). If you enable prayer reminders, the app requests prayer times from the Aladhan service using your coordinates only — no personal identifier is sent.

local.title: Where your data lives
local.body: Your main data is stored on our servers so it works across your devices and survives a lost phone. A local cache on your device keeps screens fast and usable offline. All traffic is over HTTPS.

never.title: What we'll never do
never.body: We will never sell your data, share it with advertisers, or use your worship history to target you with ads. Irtaqi uses no third-party trackers. Crash reports (Sentry) strip authentication tokens and request bodies before leaving the device.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: You can permanently delete your account from inside the app at any time (Settings → Delete account permanently). Deletion wipes your data from our servers immediately: habit logs, group memberships, points, and badges. No forms, no delays, and no recovery after deletion.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Questions about privacy? Write to privacy@irtaqi.life.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: By downloading or using Irtaqi you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, please uninstall the app and stop using the service.

service.title: What Irtaqi is
service.body: Irtaqi is a free, volunteer-built Islamic habit tracker. It helps you build and maintain daily acts of worship based on the Qur'an and the Sunnah. It is a personal productivity tool — not a religious authority, not a source of legal rulings (fatwa), and not a substitute for qualified Islamic scholarship.

account.title: Your account
account.body: You must provide an accurate email address and keep your credentials secure. You are responsible for all activity under your account. You must be at least 13 years old (or the minimum digital age in your country, whichever is higher) to register. One account per person.

content.title: Your data
content.body: Your worship logs, points, badges, and profile information belong to you. We do not license, sell, or commercially exploit them. You can delete your account and all associated data permanently at any time — see the Delete Account page for instructions.

use.title: Acceptable use
use.body: You agree not to: reverse-engineer or decompile the app; scrape or harvest data from our servers; impersonate another user or create fake accounts; use the service for any unlawful purpose; or attempt to disrupt the service in any way.

thirdparty.title: Third-party services
thirdparty.body: Irtaqi uses the Aladhan API to retrieve prayer times — your device coordinates are sent to their servers but no personal identifier is included. Crash and error reports are processed by Sentry, with authentication tokens and request bodies stripped before transmission. These services have their own terms and privacy policies.

free.title: Free service & donations
free.body: Irtaqi is and will remain free for all users. Optional donations may be accepted to cover running costs. Donations are non-refundable and do not grant any additional rights, features, or priority support.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.body: Irtaqi is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. We do not guarantee uninterrupted availability, complete accuracy of prayer times, or that the service will meet your specific requirements. Prayer times are calculated algorithmically and may differ from your local mosque's schedule — always verify with a local authority for critical obligations.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body: We may suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms. You may delete your account at any time from within the app. Termination does not affect any obligations that arose before the termination date.

changes.title: Changes to these terms
changes.body: We will update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page when these terms change. Continued use of Irtaqi after a change constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Questions about these terms? Write to us at legal@irtaqi.life.

support.title: Support
support.intro: Answers to the most common questions. If you can't find what you need, write to us directly.

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.body: Irtaqi requires iOS 16 or later on iPhone, or Android 8.0 (API 26) or later. An internet connection is required for registration and syncing; the app works offline after initial setup using a local cache.

contact.title: Still need help?
contact.body: Write to us at support@irtaqi.life and include your registered email address. We aim to respond within 2 business days.

log.q: How do I log a habit?
log.a: On the Habits tab, tap the ✓ button next to any habit to mark it done for today. Tap again to undo. For habits that involve counting (like Istighfar 100×), a counter will appear automatically. You can also open any habit for details and log it from there.

bundle.a: During onboarding, the Minimum bundle includes only the five daily prayers — a solid starting point. The Balanced bundle adds the confirmed sunnah prayers and daily adhkar. The Comprehensive bundle includes all daily habits. You can always customise manually, or adjust your selection later from Settings.

offline.q: Does the app work without internet?
offline.a: Yes. After your first login, Irtaqi stores a local copy of your habits and logs on your device. You can log worship, view stats, and browse the Qur'an reader offline. Syncing with your account resumes automatically when you reconnect.

notifMissing.q: My prayer notifications are not showing.
notifMissing.a: Open your device Settings → Notifications → Irtaqi and make sure notifications are allowed and not set to silent. On iOS, also check that Focus/Do Not Disturb isn't blocking them. Inside the app, go to Settings → Notifications and make sure prayer reminders are enabled for each prayer.

notifTime.a: Go to Settings → Location and update your location. Prayer times are calculated from your device's GPS coordinates using the Umm al-Qura (Makkah) calculation method by default. If your local mosque follows a different method or has sight-based criteria, the times may differ slightly — this is normal.

password.a: On the login screen, tap Forgot password? and enter your registered email address. You will receive a reset link. If the email doesn't arrive within a few minutes, check your spam folder. If you still can't access your account, write to support@irtaqi.life.

deleteAccount.q: How do I delete my account?
deleteAccount.a: Open Settings inside the app → scroll to the bottom → tap Delete account permanently. Deletion removes all your data from our servers immediately and cannot be undone. See the Delete Account page for full details.

joinGroup.q: How do I join a group?
joinGroup.a: Ask the group admin for the invite code, then open the Groups tab → tap Join group → enter the code. You'll be added instantly.

createGroup.q: How do I create a group?
createGroup.a: Open the Groups tab → tap Create group → choose a name. Your group's invite code is shown on the group detail screen — share it with whoever you'd like to invite.

delete.title: Delete Your Account
delete.intro: You can permanently delete your Irtaqi account and all associated data at any time — no forms, no waiting period.

inapp.title: Delete from inside the app
inapp.body: The fastest way is from within the app:

what.title: What gets deleted
what.intro: Deleting your account permanently removes the following from our servers:
what.item1: All habit logs and completion history
what.item2: Your points, level, streaks, and badges
what.item3: Group memberships and any groups you created
what.item4: Your profile information (name, email, avatar, madhhab, location)
what.item5: Any notification preferences and settings stored on the server
what.warning: Deletion is immediate and irreversible. There is no grace period and no way to recover your data after confirming.

noaccess.title: Can't access the app?
noaccess.body: If you are unable to log in or access the app, email privacy@irtaqi.life from your registered email address with the subject line "Account deletion request". We will process your request and confirm deletion within 5 business days.

steps.one: Open the app and log in.
steps.two: Go to the Settings tab (bottom navigation).
steps.three: Scroll to the bottom and tap Delete account permanently.
steps.four: Confirm when prompted. Your account and all data will be deleted immediately.

footer.made: Made with sincerity for the ummah.
